subject: Human Resources
title: The Skills Gap Crisis
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Skills Gap Crisis.

hook.text: There are 8 million unfilled jobs in the U.S.—and 6 million unemployed people. The gap isn't jobs. It's skills.

buildup.text: The skills gap is the mismatch between what employers need and what workers can offer—driven by technology outpacing education.

discovery.text: The half-life of a professional skill was 30 years in 1985. Today it's about 5 years. Skills expire faster than ever before.

twist.text: Companies blame schools. Schools blame companies. Neither is investing enough in bridging the gap that hurts both sides.

climax.text: Closing the gap requires partnerships—companies investing in training, schools teaching relevant skills, and lifelong learning.

punchline.text: The skills gap isn't a future problem. It's a current crisis hiding in plain sight.

quiz.question: What is the current half-life of a professional skill?
